title: Dogfood: review the 3 rc.2 pending entries end-to-end (>=1 approve, 1 layer-flip, 1 reject) + filesystem-edit fallback
scope: this repo's .fabric/knowledge/pending/ (live data) + dogfood-evidence.md output artifact
description: Empirical end-to-end validation: use the freshly built fab_review tool to process the 3 pending entries created during rc.2 (commit baecd5d): rc2-single-cjs-hook-across-clients.md (decisions), deepmerge-array-append-paths-for-stop-ho.md (guidelines), codex-hook-config-is-json-not-toml.md (pitfalls). Demonstrate at least 1 approve, 1 layer-flip (modify with changes.layer), 1 reject in the trail. Additionally, manually move a 4th seed file from pending/ to canonical/ to exercise the filesystem-edit fallback (TASK-005). Capture the events.jsonl trail and write to dogfood-evidence.md.

files.change: Approve to canonical .fabric/knowledge/team/decisions/KT-D-0001--rc2-single-cjs-hook-across-clients.md (assuming counter starts at 0 for KT-D)

files.target: via fab_review approve then modify with layer flip
files.change: Approve to canonical .fabric/knowledge/team/guidelines/KT-G-0001--deepmerge-array-append-paths-for-stop-ho.md, then modify with changes.layer='personal' to flip to .fabric/knowledge/personal/guidelines/KP-G-0001--deepmerge-array-append-paths-for-stop-ho.md
Reject with reason='superseded by canonical Codex hook docs in package readme; pitfall now redundant'. Pending file retained on disk per rc.3 reject semantics.

Markdown report with 4 sections: (1) Pre-state — output of fab_review action='list' before any action; (2) Action trace — chronological list of fab_review calls with action/input/output JSON; (3) Filesystem-edit fallback — manual mv of 4th file + doctor report + doctor --fix output; (4) Final events.jsonl trail — grep -E 'knowledge_(promoted|promote_started|layer_changed|rejected|deferred)' events.jsonl with line counts per event_type.

implementation: Pre-flight: ensure TASK-001..006 are merged on main; build the server (`pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server build`); confirm fab_review is registered (run mcp-inspector or call reviewKnowledge directly via test runner)
Snapshot pre-state: `cat .fabric/events.jsonl | wc -l` (record baseline); `ls .fabric/knowledge/pending/{decisions,guidelines,pitfalls}/`; record into dogfood-evidence.md Section 1

Action 1 (approve): call reviewKnowledge(repoRoot, {action:'approve', pending_paths:['.fabric/knowledge/pending/decisions/rc2-single-cjs-hook-across-clients.md']}); capture stable_id and verify git status R rename to .fabric/knowledge/team/decisions/KT-D-0001--rc2-single-cjs-hook-across-clients.md (or whatever counter resolves to); record into dogfood-evidence.md Section 2

Action 2a (approve): call reviewKnowledge(repoRoot, {action:'approve', pending_paths:['.fabric/knowledge/pending/guidelines/deepmerge-array-append-paths-for-stop-ho.md']}); capture stable_id (expect KT-G-0001)

Action 2b (modify layer flip): call reviewKnowledge(repoRoot, {action:'modify', pending_path:'.fabric/knowledge/team/guidelines/KT-G-0001--deepmerge-array-append-paths-for-stop-ho.md', changes:{layer:'personal'}}); verify response.prior_stable_id=KT-G-0001, new_stable_id=KP-G-0001; verify file moved to .fabric/knowledge/personal/guidelines/KP-G-0001--deepmerge-array-append-paths-for-stop-ho.md

Action 3 (reject): call reviewKnowledge(repoRoot, {action:'reject', pending_paths:['.fabric/knowledge/pending/pitfalls/codex-hook-config-is-json-not-toml.md'], reason:'superseded by canonical Codex hook docs in package readme; pitfall now redundant'}); verify file retained on disk; verify knowledge_rejected event in events.jsonl

Action 4 (filesystem-edit fallback): create a synthetic 4th canonical file by hand: `mkdir -p .fabric/knowledge/team/processes && cp <fixture> .fabric/knowledge/team/processes/KT-Pr-0001--manual-test.md` (NOT via fab_review approve); run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test:doctor-fix` or invoke runDoctorReport+runDoctorFix directly; verify knowledge_promoted synthesized event appears with reason='[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback'
Capture final events.jsonl trail: `tail -n 30 .fabric/events.jsonl` filtered by jq for knowledge_* events; record JSON pretty-printed into dogfood-evidence.md Section 4
Verify acceptance criteria 11 from handoff (>=1 each of promote_started/promoted, layer_changed, rejected): grep -c each in events.jsonl

Stage canonical/personal/processes file moves + dogfood-evidence.md; commit as 'chore(dogfood): rc.3 fab_review + filesystem-edit fallback dogfood (rc.3 TASK-008)' (this commit also persists the canonical knowledge entries for future use)
Note: this commit lives on main; canonical knowledge entries become real Fabric knowledge for the project

reference.pattern: Empirical dogfood pattern (rc.2 dogfood-evidence.md as sibling reference)
reference.examples: Mirror rc.2 dogfood-evidence.md sectioning (pre-state, action trace, post-state, ledger trail)

criteria: events.jsonl gains exactly 1 knowledge_promote_started + 1 knowledge_promoted from approve action 1

events.jsonl gains exactly 1 knowledge_promote_started + 1 knowledge_promoted from approve action 2a, then 1 knowledge_promote_started + 1 knowledge_layer_changed{from_layer:team,to_layer:personal} from action 2b
events.jsonl gains 1 knowledge_rejected from action 3 with reason field populated
events.jsonl gains 1 knowledge_promoted with reason starting '[synthesized]' from action 4 (filesystem-edit fallback)

agents.meta.json counters reflect: team[D]=1, team[G]=1 (allocated then layer-flipped, but counter NOT decremented per Q2 monotonic), personal[G]=1, team[Pr]=1 (from synthesized fallback OR allocated independently)
git log shows exactly 4 commits on main since rc.2: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003, ... + this dogfood commit (one atomic per task; merge order may interleave)
dogfood-evidence.md exists with all 4 sections populated and committed

rationale.chosen_approach: Empirical dogfood on this very repo's pending entries; mix of all 3 critical actions (approve/layer-flip/reject) + filesystem-edit fallback; results committed to main as canonical knowledge (the dogfood IS the project's knowledge)
alternatives_considered: Synthetic dogfood in tmpdir — rejected: missing the 'eat your own dogfood' empirical signal; rc.2 already dogfooded on real repo
Skip layer flip dogfood and rely on integration test alone — rejected: handoff acceptance criterion 11 explicitly requires layer_changed in dogfood trail
Use fab_review reject with file deletion — rejected: rc.3 reject preserves file (per TASK-002 design); deletion deferred to rc.4 doctor cleanup
decision_factors: Real-world signal value (catches CLI integration bugs that synthetic tests miss)
Resulting canonical entries become real project knowledge
rationale.tradeoffs: Dogfood mutates main branch knowledge state — irreversible without git revert. Mitigation: dogfood entry choices are on the 3 rc.2 entries that were always intended to be reviewed

manual_checks: After dogfood commit, run `fab doctor` against this repo and verify zero fixable_errors related to knowledge_* (filesystem-edit fallback already fixed)
Run `fab_review action=list` against this repo and verify pending/ now contains only the rejected entry
success_metrics: All 4 actions executed successfully without manual intervention
events.jsonl assertion of >=1 each of promote_started/promoted, layer_changed, rejected passes
dogfood-evidence.md committed alongside knowledge state changes

risks.description: If KT-D counter is not at 0 at dogfood time (rc.2 may have allocated for some reason), expected stable_ids in dogfood-evidence will be off
risks.mitigation: Read agents.meta.json BEFORE running dogfood; record actual counter values; use whatever ids are allocated (do not hard-code expected ids)
risks.fallback: Update dogfood-evidence.md after the fact with actual ids

risks.description: Layer-flipping the deepmerge guideline may not be the most semantically appropriate flip; reviewer may push back
risks.mitigation: deepMerge guideline is genuinely personal-knowledge (specific to author's preference for arrayAppendPaths config); flip is justifiable in commit message
risks.fallback: Choose different entry to layer-flip if reviewer rejects (e.g. flip the decisions entry to personal)
